subject: Human Resources
title: Promotions and Career Pathing
description: A micro-lesson that makes Promotions and Career Pathing usable.

hook.text: A top performer quits. When asked why, they say: 'I couldn't see a future here.'

buildup.text: Career pathing maps clear routes for growth—showing employees where they can go and how to get there.

discovery.text: Paths can be vertical (promotions), lateral (new roles), or skill-based (certifications and mastery levels).

twist.text: Not everyone wants to be a manager. Great career paths include individual contributor tracks too.

climax.text: Companies with visible career paths retain talent longer and develop stronger internal leadership pipelines.

punchline.text: Show people their future or they'll find one elsewhere.

quiz.question: Why should career paths include non-management tracks?
options: Not everyone wants to manage; expertise tracks retain specialists
Management is the only valid career path
It reduces the number of managers needed
